body:
This PR attempts to fix 2 bugs:

1. `xnodes`/`ynodes`/`znodes` ignored windowed indices on `Field`s

    These called e.g. `znodes(grid, loc...)` without passing the field's indices. So `znodes(Field{C,C,C}(grid, indices=(:,:,Nz)))` returned all `Nz` nodes instead of just one.

    Fixed by adding Field-specific overrides in `field.jl` that forward each dimension's index. This mirrors how `nodes(f::Field)` already correctly passed `indices=indices(f)`.

2. `mask_immersed_field!` segfaulted on windowed fields

    The masking kernel was launched with `launch!(arch, grid, :xyz, ...)`, which iterated over the full grid size `(Nx, Ny, Nz)`, even for a windowed field (e.g., `indices=(:,:,Nz)`), and that was bad when the data array only covered a single z-level because `@inbounds field[i, j, k]` accessed out-of-bounds memory.

    Fixed in `mask_immersed_field.jl` by using `KernelParameters` built from the field's `interior_indices`, which restricts iteration to exactly the windowed range.
    
Also added corresponding tests. Made with (a lot of) help from Claude.

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: Fix nodes and immersed masking for windowed fields

body:
The `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel` has switched to using model.transport_velocities to advect the tracers.
This needs to be reflected also in the `VarianceDissipationComputation`

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: Use the correct velocities in `VarianceDissipationComputations`
